source_jobs.title: Senior Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SUMMATION

Summation is building the future of business planning and analytics by bridging the gap between data and decision-making. We empower organizations to transform complex data into clear, actionable insights that drive business outcomes. Our AI-native platform integrates advanced data models with intuitive workflows, making financial performance management simple, collaborative, and effective.



THE ROLE

As a Senior Platform Engineer at Summation, you will be a key contributor to the development of our business planning and analytics platform. You will be responsible for designing, developing, and scaling the core systems that power our AI-driven solutions, focusing on performance, reliability, and customer impact. You will collaborate across teams, including product, design and deployment, to build solutions that are not only technically robust but also aligned with customer needs.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Product Development: Design and implement innovative software solutions that simplify and automate business planning and analytics. Work closely with the product and solution teams to translate business requirements into robust and scalable technical implementations.

 - Collaborative Technical Problem Solving: Tackle complex challenges involving data processing, financial modeling, and system integration, applying your technical expertise to solve problems that have a direct impact on customer success.

 - Code Quality and Best Practices: Write clean, maintainable code and perform code reviews to ensure adherence to best practices. Architect efficient and reusable frontend and backend systems that drive powerful web and mobile applications. Foster a culture of technical excellence.



QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.

 - 5+ years of experience in software engineering

 - Strong programming skills in languages such as Python, Java, or similar

 - Proficiency in SQL and experience working with data-intensive systems, including experience with financial data modeling.

 - Excellent problem-solving skills, with a focus on creating elegant, scalable solutions for complex business problems.

 - Strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.



WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary and equity options

 - Flexible (Unlimited) Paid Time Off

 - Medical, Dental, and Vision benefits for you and your family

 - 401(k) Plan

 - Parental Leave

 - A collaborative environment where your voice matters

 - Opportunities for growth and career development

 - The chance to work on cutting-edge technology that is transforming how businesses plan and execute

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SUMMATION

Summation is building the future of business planning and analytics by bridging the gap between data and decision-making. We empower organizations to transform complex data into clear, actionable insights that drive business outcomes. Our AI-native platform integrates advanced data models with intuitive workflows, making enterprise performance management simple, collaborative, and effective.




THE ROLE

As an Account Executive at Summation, you’ll be a key member of our go-to-market team, owning the full sales cycle—from prospecting to closing deals. You’ll partner closely with our founders to build and scale our sales engine, lay the groundwork for lasting client relationships, and drive our growing presence in the enterprise performance management space.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Own End-to-End Revenue

 - Generate Pipeline: Identify and target potential customers through proactive outreach and by qualifying inbound leads.

 - Close Deals: Manage the full sales cycle—from initial contact to closing—by negotiating effectively and working with stakeholder teams to finalize contracts.

Strategic Sales Enablement

 - Define the Playbook: Develop repeatable sales processes, including messaging, collateral (such as decks and case studies), and frameworks for handling objections.

 - Collaborate on Sales Strategy: Work with founders and product teams to refine our value propositions based on customer feedback and market insights.

Drive Customer & Team Success

 - Manage Relationships: Build and nurture strong connections with key decision-makers and stakeholders at our customer organizations.

 - Ensure Customer Outcomes: Partner with the Solutions team to exceed customer expectations and deliver measurable success.

 - Share Insights: Serve as the voice of the customer, informing our product roadmap and marketing strategy.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience: 4+ years in enterprise sales, preferably with a background in data or infrastructure solutions.

 - Hands-On Selling: Demonstrated success in cold outreach and lead generation, with a proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets.

 - Communication Skills: Exceptional verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to build rapport with diverse stakeholders.

 - Startup Agility: Comfortable working independently in a fast-paced, early-stage startup environment.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary and equity options

 - Flexible (Unlimited) Paid Time Off

 - Medical, Dental, and Vision benefits for you and your family

 - 401(k) Plan

 - Parental Leave

 - A collaborative environment where your voice matters

 - Opportunities for growth and career development

 - The chance to work on cutting-edge technology that is transforming how businesses plan and execute

Join us at Summation and help shape the future of data-driven decision-making.

source_jobs.title: Senior Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SUMMATION

Summation is building an AI Business Planning Platform for CFOs and finance teams. We combine a real-time modeling layer with Addison, our AI calculation engine, to turn messy enterprise data into decision-grade insights. Customers use Summation to model scenarios, explain variances, and automate weekly and monthly reviews.



THE ROLE

Design Engineering at Summation lives at the intersection of product design and frontend engineering. As a Senior Design Engineer, you will ship beautiful, high-performance product experiences in production code, lead our design system, prototype new interactions, and raise the bar on accessibility, motion, and polish. You will partner closely with Product, Design, and Engineering, moving from Figma to code to shipped features.



ROLES & RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build production UI in React and TypeScript for Summation’s web app

 - Lead the Summation Design System: tokens, components, theming, accessibility, docs, and release workflow

 - Prototype end-to-end product ideas in code, validate with real data, and graduate winners into production

 - Craft motion and micro-interactions that clarify meaning and reduce cognitive load

 - Translate flows and states from Figma into resilient, testable components with strong state management

 - Drive UI performance: bundle hygiene, code-split plans, rendering strategy, perceived latency

 - Partner with data visualization to express complex financial concepts with clarity

 - Establish guardrails: lint rules, Storybook checks, accessibility checks, visual regression in CI

 - Mentor engineers and designers on interaction patterns, system thinking, and front-of-frontend craft

 - Contribute to product quality across the stack through reviews, pairing, and high-signal documentation



YOU WILL LOVE THIS ROLE IF YOU

 - Love working across design and code, and care about the details users feel

 - Think in systems: tokens, states, variants, docs, upgrade paths

 - Prototype ideas fast and are comfortable deleting code after an experiment

 - Believe accessibility, performance, and clarity are product features

 - Enjoy collaborating with PMs, designers, and backend engineers in a high-context startup



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

 - 4+ years building production web UI with React and TypeScript

 - Strong CSS fundamentals: layout, fluid type and spacing, responsive systems, motion

 - Design system experience: tokens, component architecture, Storybook, documentation

 - Data visualization in production using one of Recharts, D3, Visx, ECharts, or similar

 - Accessibility and quality: semantic HTML, WCAG basics, keyboard and screen reader support, automated checks

 - Figma fluency: components, variants, auto-layout, dev mode, and handoff

 - Proven ability to take a concept from Figma to shipped code without heavy PM overhead



NICE TO HAVE

 - Remix or Next.js, Tailwind, shadcn/ui

 - Framer Motion for interaction design

 - WebGL or Canvas experience for advanced interactions

 - Visual regression and accessibility tooling in CI

 - Experience in fintech, analytics, or complex data products

source_jobs.title: General Application
source_jobs.description:
Don't see an open role that matches what you're looking for? Please apply and let us know what role you are interested in. We are always open to meeting talented people!



ABOUT SUMMATION

Summation is building the future of business planning and analytics by bridging the gap between data and decision-making. We empower organizations to transform complex data into clear, actionable insights that drive business outcomes. Our AI-native platform integrates advanced data models with intuitive workflows, making enterprise performance management simple, collaborative, and effective.

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SUMMATION

Summation is building the future of business planning and analytics by bridging the gap between data and decision-making. We empower organizations to transform complex data into clear, actionable insights that drive business outcomes. Our AI-native platform integrates advanced data models with intuitive workflows, making financial performance management simple, collaborative, and effective.




THE ROLE

At Summation, we are building the next generation of decision grade AI platforms. As a Forward Deployed Engineer, you are the technical owner of our client deployments, directly responsible for translating complex enterprise needs into high-value, production-ready systems. This role is highly empowering: we give you the autonomy to drive the client journey to completion and expect you to exhibit the enterprise and resourcefulness required to unblock any technical or organizational challenge. Your success is measured by the successful adoption and measurable impact of our platform on the client's business.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

The FDE role is an intersection of deep engineering, solution architecture, and client leadership. You will be fully accountable for the success of high-leverage client deployments from discovery through stable production.

 - Own End-to-End Technical Delivery: Lead and execute all phases of deployment, from initial scoping and technical design to integration, testing, and go-live.

 - Architect and Build Data Infrastructure: Design, implement, and maintain scalable data ingestion pipelines that seamlessly integrate and normalize large volumes of client financial data into the Summation platform. Core focus areas include: Designing optimized data models (SQL) and ensuring data quality meets production standards.

 - Drive Clarity and Unblock Deployments: Proactively identify, prioritize, and remove technical and organizational blockers. You don't wait for others to lead; you step in to write code, manage stakeholders, and find a way to get the job done.

 - Act as Technical Translator: Employ exceptional technical empathy to rapidly process and synthesize complex business requirements from clients (often related to accounting/finance) and translate them directly into clear platform features and engineering priorities.

 - Develop Bespoke Solutions: Design and deploy custom software agents to achieve critical, bespoke outcomes for customers, contributing directly to product innovation through your field experience.


MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

We are looking for self-starters with a demonstrated history of technical leadership and delivery.

 - Experience: Minimum 5+ years of demonstrated success in a demanding role within software engineering, data engineering, solutions architecture, or technical consulting.

 - Engineering Depth: Proven ability to write and review production-grade code (preferably in Python, Java, or comparable stacks) and a demonstrated ability to jump into the codebase to resolve complex issues or accelerate development.

 - Data Acumen: Deep familiarity with data concepts, including practical experience designing and optimizing relational schemas and writing complex, performant SQL.

 - Pipeline Experience: Solid understanding and hands-on experience related to data pipelines, analytics, or Business Intelligence (BI) systems.

 - Communication & Ownership: Must possess a proven track record of successfully managing complex projects and leading technical discussions with external business and technical stakeholders.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

These attributes indicate a candidate who is ready to accelerate their impact in the FDE role.

 - Domain Curiosity: A strong capacity and willingness to rapidly master general accounting terms and financial practices. Prior professional experience in FinTech, accounting, or finance is a significant advantage.

 - Enterprise Integration Experience: Familiarity with integrating data from major ERP, financial planning, or ledger

source_jobs.title: Staff Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SUMMATION

Summation is building the future of business planning and analytics by bridging the gap between data and decision-making. We empower organizations to transform complex data into clear, actionable insights that drive business outcomes. Our AI-native platform pairs advanced data models with intuitive workflows to make enterprise performance management simple, collaborative, and effective.




THE ROLE

As a Staff Product Engineer at Summation, you’ll be the senior front-end leader on our team, reporting directly to our CTO and owning the front-end architecture and experience of our product end to end. This is a hands-on, deeply technical role where you’ll spend your time building, not just advising, and is a role for someone with a sharp product sense who has designed and shipped polished, ambitious products from the ground up.

You’ll set the technical direction and quality bar for our front end and establish the patterns the rest of the team builds against. Our product is analytics-rich and highly interactive, which makes the front end one of the hardest and most important parts of what we do: dense, data-heavy interfaces that still need to feel fast, clear, and effortless. You’ll partner closely with the CTO, our Founding Designer, and product team to turn ambitious ideas into interfaces that perform.

As a Series A company approaching product-market fit with our first customers, we’re at the point where the front-end foundations you lay will define how well we scale. You’ll have influence over both architecture and product direction, the autonomy to make consequential decisions, and direct access to the people setting company strategy. If you’re energized by ownership, thrive in the ambiguity of an early-stage startup, and want your work to shape the product and the team around it, this is the role for you.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the front-end architecture and user experience of our product end-to-end from concept through shipping and iteration

 - Set the bar for front-end quality, patterns, and performance, and help establish the standards the team builds against

 - Partner directly with the CTO, Founding Designer, and product team to shape direction and translate ambitious ideas into polished, performant interfaces

 - Build responsive, scalable, data-dense interfaces for analytics and AI-driven workflows

 - Bring a strong product perspective to engineering decisions, advocating for the user at every step



QUALIFICATIONS

 - 8+ years of software engineering experience, including 4+ years of dedicated, hands-on front-end product development — building products, not solely platform or library work.

 - Experience building and shipping a product from zero to one with end-to-end ownership, as a founding or early engineer at a startup or with equivalent ownership inside a larger company

 - A track record of shipping to internal or external users and iterating based on their feedback

 - Strong communication and problem-solving skills, ability to work with cross-functional teams

 - Proficiency in Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, or similar languages




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE IN YOUR FIRST 6 MONTHS

 - First 30 days: You've ramped on the codebase, product, and customers, shipped your first meaningful improvements, and formed a clear point of view on the state of the front end and where it needs to go.

 - 60 days: You've identified the highest-leverage gaps in the existing front-end architecture and started addressing them, sharpening the patterns the team builds against while shipping polished, customer-facing features alongside the CTO and Founding Designer.

 - 90 days: The quality and performance bar you've raised is visible in the product, interfaces feel faster and more cohesive, and your refined patterns are becoming how the team builds by default.

 - 6 months: You're the clear owner of the front end. You've meaningfully leveled up the architecture you inherited, shipped ambitious data-d

notes: Highly technical roles, AI-native platform, and emphasis on engineering and product development suggest an engineering-heavy primary function.
